title: Tilray Brands Positioned for U.S. Expansion Amid Historic Cannabis Rescheduling
text: Tilray Brands applauded President Donald Trump's actions leading to rescheduling of cannabis from Schedule I to Schedule III under the Controlled Substances Act, which represents a fundamental shift in U.S. drug policy, unlocking significant opportunities for medical cannabis clinical research, patient access, and industry standardization. Tilray is uniquely positioned to lead the emergence of a regulated medical cannabis market. The company is among the world's largest cannabis growers with more than 7 million square feet of cultivation capacity and is a recognized global leader across pharmaceutical-grade cannabis production, clinical research, product innovation, and patient care. Tilray is actively evaluating participation in the Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation (CMMI) pilot program, which would position the Company as a supply partner to Accountable Care Organizations and oncology practices delivering hemp-derived medical cannabis to underserved and vulnerable patient populations. However, analysts caution that rescheduling is not the same as legalization, and with rescheduling it'll be easier to research marijuana in the U.S., but the border remains closed for Canadian producers moving cannabis products to the U.S.
